 {"id":"gt-8tmz","title":"Molecule Algebra: Work Composition DSL","description":"Implement the molecule algebra - a declarative language for composing, transforming, and executing structured work. Enables mechanical composition without AI.

The Three Phases: Rig → Cook → Run

- **Rig**: Source-level composition (formula YAML with extends/compose)
- **Cook**: Instantiation (formula → proto → mol/wisp)
- **Run**: Execution (agents complete steps)

Two Composition Operators:
- **Rig** operates on formulas (source level)
- **Bond** operates on artifacts (protos, mols, wisps)

Key components:
- Formulas: YAML source with composition rules (.formula.yaml)
- Cook: Pre-expand macros/aspects to flat proto
- Phase verbs (pour, wisp)
- Bond: Artifact-level polymorphic composition
- Composition operators (sequence, parallel, branch, loop, gate)
- Advice operators (before, after, around - Lisp style)
- Expansion operators (macros like Rule of Five)
- Aspects (AOP cross-cutting concerns)

See docs/rig-cook-run.md for the lifecycle spec.
See docs/molecule-algebra.md for full algebra specification.
Example formulas in .beads/formulas/","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:03:48.824827-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T19:19:52.260736-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-8tmz.1","title":"Phase verbs: pour and wisp commands","description":"Implement the phase transition verbs:
- bd pour <proto> - instantiate as persistent mol
- bd wisp <proto> - instantiate as ephemeral wisp
- Update bd mol bond to inherit phase from target

These replace the current --wisp flag with cleaner verb semantics.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:03:57.280647-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:15:11.016094-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T01:15:11.016094-08:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: bd pour, bd wisp create, and bd mol bond with phase inheritance all work correctly","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.1","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:03:57.281096-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-8tmz.12","title":"Formula parser and YAML schema","description":"Implement formula parsing from YAML:
- Define YAML schema for .formula.yaml files
- Parse steps, compose rules, vars
- Support extends for formula inheritance
- Validate formula structure

Schema should support:
- formula: name
- description: text
- version: number
- type: workflow|expansion|aspect
- vars: variable definitions
- steps: step definitions
- compose: composition rules

**Beads issue: bd-weu8**","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:20:02.788306-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T13:44:21.239181-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T13:44:21.239181-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented in Beads (bd-weu8, bd-wa2l)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.12","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:20:02.788811-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-8tmz.13","title":"bd cook command: Formula to Proto","description":"Implement the 'bd cook' command that transforms formulas into protos.

Usage: bd cook <formula-file> [options]
       bd cook .beads/formulas/*.formula.yaml

Process:
1. Parse formula YAML (uses formula parser)
2. Resolve extends (formula inheritance)
3. Expand macros (expansion rules)
4. Apply aspects (cross-cutting concerns)
5. Generate flat proto bead

Output:
- Creates proto bead in .beads/ with mol-prefix
- Proto has all steps pre-expanded
- Proto is ready for pour/wisp instantiation

**Beads issue: bd-wa2l**","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:20:03.9306-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T13:44:21.26078-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T13:44:21.26078-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented in Beads (bd-weu8, bd-wa2l)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.13","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:20:03.933113-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.13","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:20:11.133013-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-rixa","title":"Bug: parseLifecycleRequest always matches 'cycle' due to LIFECYCLE prefix","description":"In lifecycle.go, parseLifecycleRequest checks strings.Contains(title, "cycle") first, but the prefix "LIFECYCLE:" contains the word "cycle". This means ALL lifecycle messages match the cycle action, making restart and shutdown unreachable. Fix: check for restart/shutdown before cycle, or use word boundaries.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-19T16:17:16.083512-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:20:42.83084-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-rm3","title":"CLI: gt refinery commands (start, stop, status, queue)","description":"CLI commands for managing the Refinery agent.

## Commands

```bash
gt refinery start <rig>      # Start refinery for a rig
gt refinery stop <rig>       # Stop refinery
gt refinery status <rig>     # Show refinery status
gt refinery queue <rig>      # Show merge queue
```

## gt refinery start

Starts the Refinery daemon for the specified rig.

Options:
- --foreground: Run in foreground (default: background)
- --auto-merge: Enable auto-merge (default: from config)

## gt refinery stop

Stops a running Refinery. Gracefully finishes current MR if processing.

## gt refinery status

Shows:
- Running state (running/stopped)
- Current MR being processed (if any)
- Queue length
- Last merge time
- Recent activity

## gt refinery queue

Shows the merge queue:
```
Merge queue for 'wyvern':
  1. [pending] Toast/polecat-auth-fix (15m ago)
  2. [pending] Capable/polecat-new-feature (5m ago)
  
1 merged today, 0 rejected
```

## Implementation

Uses gt-ov2 (Refinery agent) for daemon functionality.","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:24.754361-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-rm3","depends_on_id":"gt-ov2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:30.679909-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}

 {"id":"gt-zde4","title":"CRITICAL: Witness hallucinated swarm work instead of spawning polecats","description":"The Witness was asked to spawn 12 polecats for a swarm. Instead of actually spawning polecats and doing the work, it:

1. Displayed 'Spawning 12 polecats...' with gt spawn commands shown as 'Waiting'
2. Then immediately showed all 12 issues as 'closed' with plausible-sounding close reasons
3. No actual polecats were spawned (gt polecat list beads shows 'No active polecats')
4. No git commits were made
5. The claimed code changes don't exist in the codebase

Example fake close reasons:
- bd-d28c: 'Added 10 tests covering createTombstone and deleteIssue wrappers with 100% coverage'
- bd-c7y5: 'Implemented --tombstones-only flag for bd compact'

Verification:
```
$ grep -r 'createTombstone' internal/rpc/*_test.go  # No output
$ grep -r 'tombstones-only' cmd/bd/*.go             # No output
$ git log --oneline --since='1 hour ago'            # No commits
```

This is a severe trust violation. The Witness needs guardrails to:
1. Actually verify polecats were spawned before reporting success
2. Verify git commits exist before closing issues
3. Never close issues it didn't actually work on","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T21:18:45.787608-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T22:28:57.33346-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T22:28:57.33346-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed via prompt rewrite: Witness CLAUDE.md now emphasizes ZFC principle (all decisions in mols), explicit 'you do NOT implement code' + 'What you never do' section, and strong 'FOLLOWING YOUR MOL' section with anti-hallucination guidance. Mol was already good - the agent just didn't follow it. Prompt now makes mol-following non-negotiable."}
